commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]
commands.repair_reason: Human review should decide whether the identity-cache contract and synthetic-only proof are enough before merge.; Cleared: The diff only changes TypeScript estimator code and colocated tests; it does not touch secrets, dependencies, CI, install scripts, publishing, or other supply-chain surfaces. (sha=03a0e995d50713da6f458c265a29fa5198ecdb7e)
commands.review_summary:
The branch adds a WeakMap-backed per-message token-pressure cache to preemptive compaction and adds six identity-cache behavior tests.

PR surface: Source +21, Tests +142. Total +163 across 2 files.

Reproducibility: yes. at source level: current main scans every message on each estimate call, so repeated precheck work is clear from the reducer. I did not rerun the synthetic benchmark or prove the reported wall-time deltas locally because this was a read-only review.
commands.review_followup: Human review should decide whether the identity-cache contract and synthetic-only proof are enough before merge.

target.title: perf(agents): memoize per-message token pressure on precheck path
target.body:
## Summary

What problem does this PR solve?

`shouldPreemptivelyCompactBeforePrompt` (the per-turn pre-prompt token-pressure precheck on the embedded-runner path) reduces over the entire transcript on every prompt submission via `estimateLlmBoundaryTokenPressure` → `estimateMessageTokenPressure`. The per-message work is recomputed every turn even when only a single message has been appended since the last call, so the wall-time cost of the precheck grows linearly with session length on the hot path before the provider request is sent.

Why does this matter now?

Tool-heavy long sessions pay multi-millisecond per-turn CPU on the precheck. Synthetic 1000-message tool-heavy transcripts measure ≈ 2.5 ms / turn; 4000-message transcripts ≈ 10 ms; a pathological synthetic shape with very large `toolCall.arguments` and tool-result text reaches ≈ 100 ms / turn at 4000 messages. None of this is dominant on its own, but it is purely pre-dispatch local CPU that the same turn pays over and over with no semantic change to the inputs.

What is the intended outcome?

Memoize `estimateMessageTokenPressure` by message object identity using a process-local `WeakMap<object, number>`. Messages on the embedded-runner precheck path are append-only and not mutated in place once added to `activeSession.messages`, so identity is a safe cache key. The reducer in `estimateLlmBoundaryTokenPressure` now calls a thin `memoizedMessageTokenPressure` wrapper that returns the cached value on a hit and computes-then-stores on a miss. No semantic change: the numerical output of `estimateLlmBoundaryTokenPressure` and the `route` / `overflowTokens` / `estimatedPromptTokens` fields of `shouldPreemptivelyCompactBeforePrompt` are byte-identical for any given input.

What is intentionally out of scope?

- Wiring `llmBoundaryTokenPressure` from a real-rendered-transport-bytes anchor (the canonical-fact-forward direction introduced in #85541). This PR caches the fallback path; it does not compete with that direction.
- `estimateMessagesTokens` in `compaction.ts` (compaction-event path) and the existing prune loop in `compaction.ts`.
- `estimateToolResultReductionPotential` in `tool-result-truncation.ts`, which is the un-cached residual cost still present in the precheck wall time.
- Anthropic-side payload / cache-control work.
- Media path work.

What does success look like?

The precheck per-turn wall time on synthetic tool-heavy long-session-shaped transcripts drops 1.9–2.0× (1.3 ms vs 2.5 ms at n=1000; 5.0 ms vs 10.1 ms at n=4000) and 5–7× on the pathological synthetic shape (3.3 ms vs 22.6 ms at n=1000; 18.4 ms vs 98.1 ms at n=4000). No semantic change, all existing tests pass, six new behavior tests pin the cache contract.

What should reviewers focus on?

- The cache key contract: identity is a safe key only because the embedded-runner path treats messages as append-only. The new test "memoizes per message identity so post-call content mutation does not alter the estimate" documents that cached estimates are tied to message identity, so runtime callers must preserve the append-only / no-in-place-mutation invariant or add explicit invalidation.
- WeakMap lifecycle: the cache is bounded by live message objects and cannot leak.
- Boundary semantics around assistant tool-call arguments, tool-result content, and image blocks are unchanged.

## Linked context

Which issue does this close?

Closes #

This PR does not close an issue. It addresses a structural cost identified in local profiling that overlaps in shape with reports such as #76702 (Windows + Feishu DM slow large-session latency) and #86231 (Codex harness prompt latency overhead). It deliberately does not claim to resolve either, because neither has been root-caused to the precheck path; the per-turn precheck saving alone is too small to explain those reports.

Which issues, PRs, or discussions are related?

Related #85541 (the PR that reshaped this estimator into `estimateLlmBoundaryTokenPressure` and introduced `llmBoundaryTokenPressure` as a caller-supplied anchor).

Was this requested by a maintainer or owner?

No.

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

- Behavior or issue addressed: per-turn `shouldPreemptivelyCompactBeforePrompt` CPU wall time on the embedded-runner precheck path scales O(N) with transcript size and recomputes the same per-message token pressure on every turn for unchanged messages.
- Real environment tested: Linux 6.8.0-111-generic, Node 22.22.2, branch `feature/openclaw-preemptive-precheck-cache` on `8da8bc4aadfc7f62af864b24718896b538c069e3`.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch:
  - `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/run/preemptive-compaction.test.ts`
  - `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/run/`
  - `node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs -p tsconfig.core.json --incremental --tsBuildInfoFile .artifacts/tsgo-cache/core.tsbuildinfo`
  - `node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs -p test/tsconfig/tsconfig.core.test.json --incremental --tsBuildInfoFile .artifacts/tsgo-cache/core-test.tsbuildinfo`
  - `node scripts/run-oxlint.mjs src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/run/preemptive-compaction.ts src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/run/preemptive-compaction.test.ts`
  - `pnpm exec oxfmt --check --threads=1 src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/run/preemptive-compaction.ts src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/run/preemptive-compaction.test.ts`
  - Local micro-benchmark script (not committed; reproducible on request).
- Evidence after fix:
  - Targeted test file: 18 tests pass (12 pre-existing + 6 new), 0 fail.
  - `src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/run/` test directory: 1681 tests pass across 102 files, 0 fail.
  - Core typecheck: exit 0. Core-test typecheck: exit 0.
  - oxlint: exit 0 on touched files. oxfmt: clean on touched files.
  - Micro-benchmark before/after on the same hardware (mean precheck wall time, 20 timed iterations + 5 warmup per case, synthetic transcripts):
    - text n=4000: 0.31 ms → 0.26 ms (1.2×).
    - tool n=1000: 2.46 ms → 1.29 ms (1.9×).
    - tool n=4000: 10.14 ms → 4.97 ms (2.0×).
    - tool-extreme n=1000: 22.56 ms → 3.27 ms (6.9×).
    - tool-extreme n=4000: 98.13 ms → 18.37 ms (5.3×).
- Observed result after fix: cold first-scan still pays the full per-message work; every subsequent scan over the same messages avoids recomputing the cached per-message estimate. The remaining measured time on the precheck path comes from uncached precheck work (`estimateToolResultReductionPotential` and the system-prompt / prompt string work), which is out of scope for this PR.
- What was not tested: no provider call, no live channel proof, no real session-log capture. The micro-benchmark uses synthetic transcripts of three shapes (text-only, tool-heavy at 2.5 KB args / 8 KB tool-result text, and a pathological tool-extreme variant at 40 KB args / 80 KB tool-result text).
- Proof limitations or environment constraints: provider API was unavailable in the test environment, so no live provider TTFT measurement was possible; the change does not depend on provider availability and is purely a local-CPU optimization on the pre-dispatch path.

## Tests and validation

Which commands did you run?

Listed under "Exact steps or command run after this patch" above.

What regression coverage was added or updated?

Six new behavior tests in `src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/run/preemptive-compaction.test.ts`, all inside a new `describe("per-message identity cache", …)` block:

1. Same boundary estimate on repeat scans of the same messages.
2. Memoizes per message identity so post-call content mutation does not alter the estimate (documents that cached estimates are tied to message identity, so callers must preserve append-only / no-in-place-mutation or add invalidation).
3. Re-counts when a brand-new message identity is appended.
4. Does not double-count when the same message identity appears twice.
5. Preserves precheck `route` / `overflowTokens` / `estimatedPromptTokens` decisions across repeat calls.
6. Counts assistant tool-call argument bytes identically on repeat scans (covers the heavy-`JSON.stringify` path).

What failed before this fix, if known?

No prior test failures. The change is a performance optimization, not a correctness fix.

If no test was added, why not?

N/A.

## Risk checklist

Did user-visible behavior change? (`Yes/No`)

No. `shouldPreemptivelyCompactBeforePrompt` returns byte-identical decisions for any given input. The only observable difference is reduced wall time on the precheck path in long tool-heavy sessions.

Did config, environment, or migration behavior change? (`Yes/No`)

No.

Did security, auth, secrets, network, or tool execution behavior change? (`Yes/No`)

No.

What is the highest-risk area?

The cache key contract. If a future contributor introduces in-place mutation of messages on the embedded-runner precheck path, `memoizedMessageTokenPressure` would return a stale value. Today the path is append-only.

How is that risk mitigated?

The new test "memoizes per message identity so post-call content mutation does not alter the estimate" documents the cache-tied-to-identity invariant. The doc-comment block above `messageTokenPressureCache` names the same invariant in code. Process-local metadata caches are explicitly permitted by the repo `AGENTS.md` ("Process-local metadata caches ok when lifecycle-owned and bounded/single-slot. Freshness exceptions need named owner + tests"); this cache is process-local, lifecycle-bounded by the WeakMap-to-message reference, single-slot per message, and covered by the named test contract.

## Current review state

What is the next action?

Maintainer review.

What is still waiting on author, maintainer, CI, or external proof?

Maintainer review.

Which bot or reviewer comments were addressed?

Pre-submission self-review applied: removed local shorthand, softened benchmark wording from "representative" to "synthetic … long-session-shaped", clarified the "near zero per-message CPU" claim to match the measured table, removed a non-diff line reference, and reframed the cache-mutation test as documenting the identity-key invariant rather than asserting a regression-failure.
target.author: masatohoshino
